matches.match_rationale: The absence of clear, standardized promotion criteria documentation is a core driver of inconsistency. AI text generation can produce a canonical, plain-language promotion policy document from structured inputs (thresholds, weightings, exception rules), which can then be versioned and distributed across all campuses. This directly addresses the parent/student clarity gap and the ad hoc special-case handling by giving staff a reference document they can cite.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A policy generation tool where curriculum leads input grade-level parameters and AI drafts a unified promotion criteria handbook — including assessment weightings, attendance thresholds, and exception pathways — formatted for both staff and parent communication.

matches.match_rationale: Designing a promotion framework that satisfies multiple simultaneous constraints — minimum grade thresholds, attendance floors, assessment weighting, campus-level variance allowances, and exception criteria — is a classic constraint satisfaction problem. AI can enumerate valid policy configurations and flag contradictions before rollout, replacing the current ad hoc approach where each campus independently interprets ambiguous rules.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A configuration tool where a central academic team defines the constraint set (e.g., 'assessment weight must be 60-80%', 'attendance floor between 70-85%') and AI generates all compliant policy variants, enabling deliberate standardization rather than accidental divergence.

matches.match_rationale: Once a standardized policy exists, applying it consistently is the next failure mode — promotion decisions still get made ad hoc because staff interpret borderline cases differently. A structured classification model trained on student feature vectors (grades per subject, attendance rate, assessment scores) can produce a promotion recommendation (promote / borderline / retain) that enforces the same decision logic across all campuses, reducing reviewer subjectivity.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An end-of-term promotion decision support tool that ingests student academic records and outputs a recommendation with supporting rationale, flagging borderline cases for human review while auto-approving clear-cut promote and retain decisions.

matches.match_rationale: A significant portion of the pain is experienced by parents and students who cannot get a straight answer about promotion requirements or the status of a borderline case. A conversational agent grounded in the official promotion policy can answer multi-turn questions ('What does my child need to pass? What happens if attendance is 72%?') in plain language, reducing disputes and staff time spent on repetitive parent inquiries.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A parent-facing promotion advisory chatbot embedded in the school portal, able to explain the promotion criteria, walk through a student's current standing against thresholds, and outline next steps — escalating to a human counselor only for documented exception cases.
